steps.title: Find the reversal
steps.content: The first sentence says a parent may have a trait while none of that parent's children inherit it. The next sentence says the grandchildren could still inherit the trait. The transition must signal contrast despite the previous statement.

steps.title: Reject additive signals
steps.content: B would introduce a more specific detail, while C and D add information in the same direction. The needed transition means "despite that," which fits the trait skipping one generation and appearing in the next. The answer is A.
